iterations.theme: Osobni rast, partnerski odnosi i rani izazovi očinstva

sections.subtitle: Djeca Uče Primjerom: Izazov Roditeljske Autentičnosti
sections.screenshot_description: Voditeljica objašnjava kako djeca oponašaju roditelje, a ne samo slušaju njihove riječi.
sections.content: Voditeljica podcasta 'Rastući s djecom' u uvodu ističe ključnu pedagošku istinu: djeca ne rade ono što im se kaže, već ono što vide da roditelji rade. Postavlja se pitanje koliko roditelji zaista žive vrijednosti koje žele prenijeti na svoju djecu. Primjeri poput roditelja koji psuje, a djetetu brani psovanje, ili se skriva u kuhinji jedući čokoladu dok djetetu brani slatkiše, jasno ilustriraju ovaj jaz između riječi i djela. Naglašava se važnost samorefleksije i spremnosti na promjenu kako bi roditelji bili autentičan primjer. U tom kontekstu, predstavljen je gost, **Marko Matijašević**, obiteljski čovjek, otac dvoje djece, suprug, teolog i autor knjige 'Slomljen, ali blagoslovljen', koji je poznat po svojoj otvorenosti u dijeljenju osobnih borbi.
sections.subtitle_en: Children Learn by Example: The Challenge of Parental Authenticity
sections.screenshot_description_en: The host explains how children imitate parents, and not just listen to their words.
sections.content_en: The host of the podcast 'Growing with Children' in the introduction highlights a key pedagogical truth: children do not do what they are told, but what they see their parents do. The question arises how much parents truly live the values they want to pass on to their children. Examples such as a parent who curses, but forbids the child from cursing, or hides in the kitchen eating chocolate while forbidding sweets to the child, clearly illustrate this gap between words and deeds. The importance of self-reflection and readiness for change is emphasized so that parents can be an authentic example. In this context, the guest, **Marko Matijašević**, a family man, father of two children, husband, theologian, and author of the book 'Broken, but Blessed', who is known for his openness in sharing personal struggles, was introduced.

sections.subtitle: Otvorenost o Borbama: Marko Matijašević kao Relatabilan Autoritet
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o važnosti dijeljenja osobnih borbi kako bi bio bliži ljudima.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević otvoreno priznaje da nema problema s dijeljenjem svojih osobnih borbi. Njegova motivacija leži u želji da se približi ljudima i pokaže da nije 'nedostižan autoritet' ili netko tko stvara lažnu sliku savršenstva. Svoje osobne izazove i poteškoće integrira u svoje propovijedi i predavanja, jer vjeruje da svi ljudi prolaze kroz slične borbe, bez obzira na njihovu ulogu ili iskustvo. Ističe da čak i duhovni autoriteti nisu imuni na padove i probleme, te da dijeljenje tih iskustava stvara dublju povezanost s publikom.
sections.subtitle_en: Openness about Struggles: Marko Matijašević as a Relatable Authority
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about the importance of sharing personal struggles in order to be closer to people.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević openly admits that he has no problem with sharing his personal struggles. His motivation lies in the desire to get closer to people and show that he is not an 'unattainable authority' or someone who creates a false image of perfection. He integrates his personal challenges and difficulties into his sermons and lectures, because he believes that all people go through similar struggles, regardless of their role or experience. He points out that even spiritual authorities are not immune to falls and problems, and that sharing these experiences creates a deeper connection with the audience.

sections.subtitle: Put Promjene i Vjere: Od Prisile do Predanosti
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava kako je njegov put vjere započeo prisilom, a prerastao u predanost.
sections.content: Voditeljica pita Marka o njegovom osobnom putu promjene i vjere. Marko objašnjava da njegov put nije bio rezultat nekog dramatičnog pada ili slomljenosti, već postepenog procesa. U srednjoj školi mu je, kako kaže, 'sve bilo super', imao je društvo i život kakav je želio. Odvajanje od tog svjetovnog života zbog vjere doživio je kao žrtvu. Njegova majka ga je u početku 'prisiljavala' da ide na molitvene zajednice, ali s vremenom je sam osjetio želju da se uključi. Od redovitog dolaska, postao je član, zatim voditelj mladih. Ovaj put služenja i predanosti traje već 15 godina. Zanimljivo je da je Marko, unatoč tome što nije volio pisati i hrvatski jezik u školi, napisao knjigu, što svjedoči o dubokoj osobnoj preobrazbi.
sections.subtitle_en: Path of Change and Faith: From Coercion to Devotion
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains how his path of faith began by coercion and grew into devotion.
sections.content_en: The host asks Marko about his personal path of change and faith. Marko explains that his path was not the result of some dramatic fall or brokenness, but a gradual process. In high school, as he says, 'everything was great' for him, he had friends and the life he wanted. He experienced separating from that worldly life because of faith as a sacrifice. His mother initially 'forced' him to go to prayer communities, but over time he himself felt the desire to get involved. From regularly attending, he became a member, then a youth leader. This path of service and dedication has lasted for 15 years. It is interesting that Marko, despite not liking writing and the Croatian language in school, wrote a book, which testifies to a deep personal transformation.

sections.subtitle: Supruga kao Korektiv: Uloga Mirjane u Markovom Javnom Nastupu
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o ulozi svoje supruge Mirjane u njegovom javnom nastupu i pisanju.
sections.content: Voditeljica se našalila pitajući je li Markova supruga napisala njegovu knjigu. Marko objašnjava da je njegova supruga, **Mirjana**, profesorica hrvatskog jezika i voditeljica škole govorništva 'Heleka', lektorirala knjigu i davala mu ideje i sugestije. Ističe da ga je Mirjana, koju je upoznao u zajednici, ispravljala u javnom nastupu od samih početaka. Njezina uloga korektiva bila je ključna u njegovom razvoju kao govornika, a on se šali da je ona 'ljubomorna' što on gostuje bez nje. Ova dinamika pokazuje kako se profesionalne vještine njegove supruge prepliću s njegovim javnim djelovanjem.
sections.subtitle_en: Wife as Corrective: Mirjana's Role in Marko's Public Appearance
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about the role of his wife Mirjana in his public appearance and writing.
sections.content_en: The host joked asking if Marko's wife wrote his book. Marko explains that his wife, **Mirjana**, a Croatian language professor and head of the 'Heleka' public speaking school, proofread the book and gave him ideas and suggestions. He points out that Mirjana, whom he met in the community, corrected him in public speaking from the very beginning. Her role as a corrective was crucial in his development as a speaker, and he jokes that she is 'jealous' that he is a guest without her. This dynamic shows how his wife's professional skills intertwine with his public activity.

sections.subtitle: Upornost u Ljubavi: Priča o Osvajanju Mirjaninog Srca
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević priča o svojoj upornosti u vezi s Mirjanom, koja ga je odbijala godinama.
sections.content: Voditeljica se dotiče Markove upornosti u vezi, opisujući je kao 'uporni pokušaj' umjesto 'ljubavi na prvi pogled'. Marko potvrđuje da je čekao Mirjanu četiri do pet godina. Upoznao ju je u zajednici, gdje je vodila slavljenički tim. Iako mu se odmah svidjela, ona nije dijelila početni entuzijazam, sanjajući o 'visokom, crnom, možda pjevaču'. Marko je ustrajao unatoč njezinim odbijanjima, gotovo odustajući nakon četiri godine. Međutim, nakon što je dobila 'potice' da mu pruži priliku, Marko je pokušao još jednom, i tako je njihova veza započela 2015. godine. Marko povlači paralelu između svoje upornosti i majčine 'prisile' da ide na molitvene susrete, ističući da su oba pristupa, iako nekonvencionalna, urodila pozitivnim plodovima. Savjetuje roditeljima da 'plate' djeci odlazak na duhovne susrete, naglašavajući da se tamo može dogoditi nešto što će ih potaknuti na promjenu. Ipak, napominje da je njegova upornost bila moguća jer je bio mlad (19 godina) i imao je vremena čekati, te da to nije univerzalni recept za sve.
sections.subtitle_en: Perseverance in Love: The Story of Conquering Mirjana's Heart
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević talks about his persistence regarding Mirjana, who rejected him for years.
sections.content_en: The host touches upon Marko's persistence in the relationship, describing it as 'persistent attempt' instead of 'love at first sight'. Marko confirms that he waited for Mirjana for four to five years. He met her in the community, where she led the worship team. Although he immediately liked her, she did not share his initial enthusiasm, dreaming of 'a tall, dark, perhaps a singer'. Marko persisted despite her rejections, almost giving up after four years. However, after she received 'nudges' to give him a chance, Marko tried one more time, and thus their relationship began in 2015. Marko draws a parallel between his persistence and his mother's 'coercion' to go to prayer meetings, emphasizing that both approaches, although unconventional, yielded positive fruits. He advises parents to 'pay' for their children to go to spiritual meetings, emphasizing that something can happen there that will encourage them to change. Nevertheless, he notes that his persistence was possible because he was young (19 years old) and had time to wait, and that this is not a universal recipe for everyone.

sections.subtitle: Dubina naspram Površnosti: Predanost u Modernim Odnosima
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o površnosti modernih odnosa i važnosti predanosti.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević razmišlja o površnosti modernih odnosa. Iako u svom krugu mladih primjećuje ozbiljan pristup vezama s ciljem trajnog partnerstva, priznaje da općenito postoji trend lakog mijenjanja partnera. Upozorava mlade da svaka veza ostavlja trag i 'oštećuje' dio srca, stoga je važno biti oprezan. Kritizira zabludu da će 'moja ljubav promijeniti osobu', ističući da je ključno prihvatiti partnera takvog kakav jest. Kao primjer navodi rečenicu svoje supruge Mirjane: 'Marko, da ti ništa ne promijeniš do kraja života, ja sam spremna živjeti s tobom.' Ova rečenica služi mu kao mjerilo za savjetovanje mladih o tome jesu li spremni prihvatiti partnerove mane do kraja života. Naglašava da se veza i brak često razlikuju, te da se u braku često vraćamo obrascima ponašanja naučenim od roditelja. Stoga je ključno razgovarati o važnim temama poput financija, odnosa s obiteljima i prioriteta prije ulaska u brak. Spominje i 'jezike ljubavi', gdje se njegovi 'čini služenja' sudaraju s Mirjaninim 'kvalitetnim vremenom', što može dovesti do nerazumijevanja i osjećaja nezadovoljstva unatoč trudu.
sections.subtitle_en: Depth versus Superficiality: Commitment in Modern Relationships
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about the superficiality of modern relationships and the importance of commitment.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević reflects on the superficiality of modern relationships. Although in his circle of young people he observes a serious approach to relationships with the goal of a lasting partnership, he admits that generally there is a trend of easily changing partners. He warns young people that every relationship leaves a trace and 'damages' a part of the heart, therefore it is important to be careful. He criticizes the delusion that 'my love will change the person', emphasizing that it is crucial to accept the partner as they are. As an example, he cites a sentence from his wife Mirjana: 'Marko, even if you don't change anything for the rest of your life, I am ready to live with you.' This sentence serves him as a measure for advising young people on whether they are ready to accept their partner's flaws for the rest of their lives. He emphasizes that a relationship and marriage often differ, and that in marriage we often return to patterns of behavior learned from parents. Therefore, it is crucial to discuss important topics such as finances, relationships with families, and priorities before entering marriage. He also mentions 'love languages', where his 'acts of service' clash with Mirjana's 'quality time', which can lead to misunderstanding and feelings of dissatisfaction despite the effort.

sections.subtitle: Sponzorska poruka: HIP Baby proizvodi
sections.screenshot_description: Prikaz Hip Baby proizvoda za njegu beba.
sections.content: Kupanje je više od rutine, to je mali ritual nježnosti. Hip Baby zamft pjena za pranje, s ekstraktom biobadema, nježno čisti lice i ruke već od prvog dana života. Bez sapuna, pH neutralna i ne peče oči, savršena za osjetljivu dječju kožu. Sada uz omiljenu žutu patkicu stižu i limitirana izdanja: Ružičasta vila i Plavi muzičar. Hip, nježna njega, sada nježnija nego ikad.
sections.subtitle_en: Sponsor message: HIP Baby products
sections.screenshot_description_en: Overview of Hip Baby products for baby care.
sections.content_en: Bathing is more than a routine, it is a small ritual of tenderness. Hip Baby zamft washing foam, with organic almond extract, gently cleanses face and hands already from the first day of life. Without soap, pH neutral and does not sting eyes, perfect for sensitive baby skin. Now with the favorite yellow duck, limited editions also arrive: Pink Fairy and Blue Musician. Hip, gentle care, now gentler than ever.

sections.subtitle: Brak i Osobni Rast: Prihvaćanje Promjena i Sazrijevanja
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o tome kako se on i njegova supruga mijenjaju kroz brak i roditeljstvo.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević ističe kako se on sam značajno promijenio od ulaska u brak i rođenja djece. Njegova supruga Mirjana često primjećuje te promjene, a Marko objašnjava da su se njegove odgovornosti proširile s fakulteta i izlazaka na posao, dvoje djece i hipoteku. Težina i brige koje ga opterećuju danas su neusporedive s onima od prije devet godina. Naglašava da je promjena neizbježna u braku i da je ključno prihvatiti te promjene. Kritizira one koji odustaju od braka jer se partner promijenio, ističući da je važno željeti dobro jedno drugome i shvatiti da su partneri tim. Brak i odnos moraju imati svoj tijek i promjene, a partneri moraju biti spremni prihvatiti sazrijevanje i rasti zajedno.
sections.subtitle_en: Marriage and Personal Growth: Acceptance of Changes and Maturation
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about how he and his wife are changing through marriage and parenthood.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević points out how he himself significantly changed since entering marriage and the birth of children. His wife Mirjana often notices these changes, and Marko explains that his responsibilities expanded from college and going out to work, two children, and a mortgage. The weight and worries that burden him today are incomparable to those from nine years ago. He emphasizes that change is inevitable in marriage and that it is crucial to accept these changes. He criticizes those who give up on marriage because their partner changed, pointing out that it is important to wish each other well and understand that partners are a team. Marriage and the relationship must have their course and changes, and partners must be ready to accept maturing and grow together.

sections.subtitle: Razumijevanje Partnera u Promjeni: Izazov Bez Osuđivanja
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava kako pristupiti partneru koji ne prati osobne promjene, s naglaskom na razumijevanje.
sections.content: Voditeljica postavlja pitanje o situacijama kada jedna osoba u braku prolazi kroz osobnu promjenu, dok druga to ne razumije. Marko Matijašević naglašava da je ključno imati **razumijevanja** za partnera koji ne prati promjene. Osoba koja se mijenja doživjela je 'prosvjetljenje' ili iskustvo koje druga osoba nije. Upozorava da se ne smije 'zlostavljati' partnera vlastitom promjenom, čineći ga da se osjeća 'čudno' ili 'pogrešno'. Kao primjer navodi situacije kada se jedna strana previše posveti vjeri, a drugu stranu to zbunjuje ili odbija. Marko ističe da prava promjena treba učiniti osobu boljom, strpljivijom i razumijevajućom, tako da partner osjeti korist od te promjene. Povlači paralelu s Isusom, koji je činio da se 'grešnici' osjećaju prihvaćeno, za razliku od farizeja koji su osuđivali. Ako osobni rast izaziva osudu kod drugih, to znači da se ne slijedi Krist, već farizeji. Stoga, Marko zaključuje da osobna promjena treba voditi većoj mudrosti i strpljenju prema partneru, razumijevanju njihovih pogleda i osjećaja.
sections.subtitle_en: Understanding Partner in Change: Challenge Without Judgment
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains how to approach a partner who does not keep up with personal changes, with an emphasis on understanding.
sections.content_en: The host asks a question about situations when one person in a marriage goes through a personal change, while the other does not understand it. Marko Matijašević emphasizes that it is crucial to have **understanding** for a partner who does not follow the changes. The person who is changing has experienced 'enlightenment' or an experience that the other person has not. He warns that one must not 'abuse' a partner with one's own change, making them feel 'strange' or 'wrong'. As an example, he cites situations when one side dedicates too much to faith, and this confuses or repels the other side. Marko points out that true change should make a person better, more patient, and more understanding, so that the partner feels the benefit of that change. He draws a parallel with Jesus, who made 'sinners' feel accepted, unlike the Pharisees who condemned. If personal growth causes condemnation in others, it means that Christ is not being followed, but rather the Pharisees. Therefore, Marko concludes that personal change should lead to greater wisdom and patience towards the partner, understanding their views and feelings.

sections.subtitle: Molitvene Zajednice i Obitelj: Uključivanje Djece u Duhovni Život
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o tome kako uključuje svoju djecu u aktivnosti molitvene zajednice.
sections.content: Voditeljica pita je li molitvena zajednica ponekad bijeg od obiteljskih odgovornosti. Marko Matijašević priznaje da zajednice mogu biti privlačne zbog prihvaćanja, ali naglašava da je ključno kako se obitelj odnosi prema tome. U njegovoj obitelji, djeca su **uključena** u aktivnosti zajednice. Ne želi da djeca dožive zajednicu kao nešto što im oduzima roditelje. Dijeli primjer voditelja zajednice iz Londona koji je savjetovao roditeljima da provode sat vremena dnevno s djecom, radeći ono što djeca žele. Na taj način, zajednica postaje nešto što 'vraća' roditelje djeci, a ne ih oduzima. Marko želi da njegova djeca vide zajednicu kao pozitivan dio života, a ne kao kaznu. Ističe da duhovni rast treba biti vidljiv i kod kuće, čineći roditelje strpljivijima i boljima. Priznaje da je izazovno, jer se kod kuće osjećamo slobodnima 'otpustiti ventile', ali rad na sebi treba se odraziti i na obiteljske odnose.
sections.subtitle_en: Prayer Communities and Family: Involving Children in Spiritual Life
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about how he includes his children in the activities of the prayer community.
sections.content_en: The host asks if a prayer community is sometimes an escape from family responsibilities. Marko Matijašević admits that communities can be attractive due to acceptance, but emphasizes that it is crucial how the family relates to it. In his family, children are **included** in community activities. He does not want children to experience the community as something that takes their parents away from them. He shares an example of a community leader from London who advised parents to spend an hour a day with their children, doing what the children want. In this way, the community becomes something that 'returns' parents to their children, and does not take them away. Marko wants his children to see the community as a positive part of life, and not as a punishment. He points out that spiritual growth should be visible at home too, making parents more patient and better. He admits that it is challenging, because at home we feel free to 'let off steam', but self-work should also be reflected in family relationships.

sections.subtitle: Duhovni Zakon Sjetve: Strpljenje i Obilje u Roditeljstvu
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava duhovni zakon sjetve u kontekstu roditeljstva.
sections.content: Voditeljica naglašava važnost roditeljskog primjera i strpljenja, ističući da se plodovi rada možda neće vidjeti odmah, već tek za dvadeset godina. Marko Matijašević se nadovezuje na to spominjući 'duhovni zakon sjetve', prema kojem samo četvrtina sjemena uspije donijeti plod, ali taj plod je iznimno obilan – tridesetostruk, šezdesetostruk, pa čak i stostruk. Naglašava da je roditeljski zadatak neprestano 'bacati sjeme', odnosno ulagati ljubav, pažnju i prisutnost, čak i ako se rezultati ne vide odmah. Vjeruje da se obilna ljubav i poštovanje koje se daju djeci sigurno vraćaju, stvarajući temelj za snažan i povjerljiv odnos.
sections.subtitle_en: Spiritual Law of Sowing: Patience and Abundance in Parenthood
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains the spiritual law of sowing in the context of parenthood.
sections.content_en: The host emphasizes the importance of parental example and patience, pointing out that the fruits of labor may not be seen immediately, but only after twenty years. Marko Matijašević builds on this by mentioning the 'spiritual law of sowing', according to which only a quarter of the seed manages to bear fruit, but that fruit is exceptionally abundant – thirtyfold, sixtyfold, and even a hundredfold. He emphasizes that the parental task is to constantly 'sow seed', that is, to invest love, attention, and presence, even if the results are not immediately visible. He believes that the abundant love and respect given to children surely return, creating a foundation for a strong and trusting relationship.

sections.subtitle: Očinstvo kao Izazov Samom Sebi: Djeca kao Ogledalo Roditelja
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o tome kako su mu djeca najveći izazov i ogledalo za osobni rast.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević ističe da je najveći izazov u očinstvu on sam sebi. Smatra da djeca upijaju i kopiraju ponašanje roditelja, stoga je njegov glavni zadatak modelirati željeno ponašanje, a ne samo govoriti. Prisjeća se situacije kada je njegov dvogodišnji sin ponovio ponašanje koje mu se nije svidjelo, što ga je osvijestilo o tome koliko ga djeca promatraju. Kroz fotografije na kojima ga sin gleda dok drži predavanja ili potpisuje knjige, Marko shvaća da ga sinov pogled tjera na promjenu. Djeca su mu ogledalo koje mu pokazuje vlastite obrasce i probleme. Marko želi riješiti svoje unutarnje borbe sada kako bi izbjegao probleme u odnosu sa sinovima u budućnosti. Navodi primjer svoje borbe s osjećajem odbačenosti, gdje bi se, u slučaju odbijanja, povukao iz odnosa. Shvatio je da bi to mogao prenijeti na svoje sinove i da mora riješiti taj obrazac kako ne bi narušio njihov odnos za 15 godina. Kroz ovaj proces, Marko se bavi sobom kako bi bio bolji otac.
sections.subtitle_en: Fatherhood as a Challenge to Oneself: Children as a Mirror of Parents
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about how his children are the biggest challenge and mirror for personal growth.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević points out that the biggest challenge in fatherhood is himself. He believes that children absorb and copy the behavior of their parents, therefore his main task is to model desired behavior, and not just speak. He recalls a situation when his two-year-old son repeated behavior he did not like, which made him aware of how much children observe him. Through photographs in which his son looks at him while he gives lectures or signs books, Marko realizes that his son's gaze compels him to change. Children are a mirror to him that shows him his own patterns and problems. Marko wants to resolve his internal struggles now to avoid problems in his relationship with his sons in the future. He cites an example of his struggle with the feeling of rejection, where, in case of rejection, he would withdraw from a relationship. He realized that he could pass this on to his sons and that he must resolve this pattern so as not to damage their relationship in 15 years. Through this process, Marko deals with himself to be a better father.

sections.subtitle: Prenošenje Obrazaca i Strahova: Lekcije iz Prošlosti
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević priča anegdotu o prenošenju straha od novca s generacije na generaciju.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević dijeli priču o propovjedniku čija je baka, preživjevši Veliku depresiju u Americi, usadila u njega strah od novca i dugova. Taj strah ga je tjerao da radi više poslova i uvijek štedi. Kasnije, njegova kćerka je, na rođendanskom ručku, pokazala anksioznost zbog cijene burgera od samo dva dolara, što je ocu otkrilo da je na nju prenio svoj obrazac straha od financija. Ova priča Marku služi kao podsjetnik da mora biti svjestan vlastitih obrazaca ponašanja i strahova koje nesvjesno prenosi na svoju djecu. Ističe da se mijenja zbog njih, želeći da oni vole Isusa više nego njega i da ga slušaju ako se njihovi savjeti razlikuju. To smatra najvećim uspjehom u roditeljstvu – usaditi im duboku vjeru i odnos s Bogom. Marko želi da njegova djeca upijaju vrijednosti i spoznaje koje će im pomoći da se razvijaju i donose vlastite odluke, svjestan da će se suočiti s izazovima i utjecajima društva.
sections.subtitle_en: Transmission of Patterns and Fears: Lessons from the Past
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević tells an anecdote about the transmission of fear of money from generation to generation.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević shares a story about a preacher whose grandmother, having survived the Great Depression in America, instilled in him a fear of money and debts. That fear drove him to work multiple jobs and always save. Later, his daughter, at a birthday lunch, showed anxiety over the price of a burger of only two dollars, which revealed to the father that he had transferred his pattern of fear of finances to her. This story serves Marko as a reminder that he must be aware of his own patterns of behavior and fears that he unconsciously transfers to his children. He emphasizes that he is changing because of them, wanting them to love Jesus more than him and that they listen to him if their advice differs. He considers that the greatest success in parenting – to instill in them a deep faith and relationship with God. Marko wants his children to absorb values and insights that will help them develop and make their own decisions, aware that they will face challenges and societal influences.

iterations.theme_en: Personal growth, partner relationships and early challenges of fatherhood

iterations.theme: Roditeljski izazovi, odgoj i duhovne vrijednosti

sections.subtitle: Roditeljski izazovi: Kako se nositi s dječjim nezadovoljstvom i vlastitim 'okidačima'
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava kako ga dječje nezadovoljstvo 'trigerira'.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević detaljno opisuje izazove roditeljstva, posebno se osvrćući na 'loše dane' i dječje nezadovoljstvo. Ističe kako njegov sin, unatoč tome što mu je sve posloženo, često pronalazi razloge za nezadovoljstvo. Kao primjer navodi situaciju kada je sin plakao jer prijatelj nije sjedio s njima u autu, iako su cijeli tjedan molili da idu kod tog prijatelja. Marko priznaje da ga takve situacije 'trigeriraju', budeći u njemu osjećaje nezahvalnosti i preispitivanja je li mogao učiniti više.

Međutim, Marko naglašava da takve situacije vidi kao priliku za osobni rast. Uči se podnijeti tuđu emociju i odvojiti se od vlastite potrebe da uvijek zadovolji sve. Priznaje da se ponekad izdere, ali ključnim smatra ono što radi *nakon* toga: iskomunicira situaciju, ispriča se i objasni svoje ponašanje. Podsjeća na Isusovu rečenicu Petru: 'Kad dođeš k sebi, ohrabri braću svoju', naglašavajući važnost pomirenja i razgovora nakon svađe.

Marko također spominje kako ponekad pusti sina da se 'svađa' s njim, želeći da se nauči izboriti za sebe. Prisjeća se i situacije kada je sin htio sam izaći iz auta i ozlijedio se, a on je shvatio da mu je nametnuo etikete 'ne možeš' i 'treba ti pomoć', koje inače skida s odraslih. Povezuje to s križnim putem i Isusovim padovima, ističući da je u redu pasti i tražiti pomoć. Važno je da djeca znaju da je u redu ne uspjeti i nositi se s time. Kao primjer navodi i vožnju iz vrtića u Zagrebu, kada je umoran i nervozan, pa djeci kaže da je ljut i treba tišinu, ali da će kasnije razgovarati. Zaključuje da uspjeh u partnerskim odnosima i odgoju nije u izbjegavanju svađa, već u načinu na koji se one rješavaju i kako se partneri odnose *između* sukoba.
sections.subtitle_en: Parental challenges: How to deal with children's dissatisfaction and one's own 'triggers'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains how child's dissatisfaction 'triggers' him.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević describes in detail the challenges of parenthood, especially referring to 'bad days' and children's dissatisfaction. He points out how his son, despite everything being arranged for him, often finds reasons for dissatisfaction. As an example, he cites a situation when his son cried because a friend did not sit with them in the car, even though they had begged all week to go to that friend's house. Marko admits that such situations 'trigger' him, awakening in him feelings of ingratitude and questioning whether he could have done more. However, Marko emphasizes that he sees such situations as an opportunity for personal growth. He learns to bear another's emotion and to separate himself from his own need to always satisfy everyone. He admits that he sometimes yells, but considers what he does *after* that to be key: he communicates the situation, apologizes, and explains his behavior. He recalls Jesus' sentence to Peter: 'When you have turned back, strengthen your brothers,' emphasizing the importance of reconciliation and conversation after an argument. Marko also mentions how he sometimes lets his son 'argue' with him, wanting him to learn to stand up for himself. He also recalls a situation when his son wanted to get out of the car by himself and got injured, and he realized that he had imposed on him the labels 'you can't' and 'you need help', which he otherwise removes from adults. He connects this with the Way of the Cross and Jesus' falls, emphasizing that it is okay to fall and ask for help. It is important for children to know that it is okay not to succeed and to deal with it. As an example, he also cites a drive from kindergarten in Zagreb, when he is tired and nervous, so he tells the children that he is angry and needs silence, but that they will talk later. He concludes that success in partnerships and upbringing is not in avoiding arguments, but in the way they are resolved and how partners relate *between* conflicts.

sections.subtitle: Sponzorska poruka: HIP Gritskalice
sections.screenshot_description: Prikaz HIP Gritskalica.
sections.content: Mali istraživači trebaju velike, ali zdrave pauze. HIP Gritskalice su uravnoteženi međuobroci od pažljivo odabranih bio sastojaka, bez dodanih aroma i sa slatkoćom isključivo iz voća. Hrskave, lagane i prilagođene malim ručicama, idealne su za dom, vrtić ili parkić. Zabavne su za djecu, a ukusne i za odrasle.
sections.subtitle_en: Sponsor message: HIP Gritskalice
sections.screenshot_description_en: Display HIP Gritskalica.
sections.content_en: Little explorers need big, but healthy breaks. HIP Gritskalice are balanced snacks from carefully selected organic ingredients, without added flavors and with sweetness exclusively from fruit. Crunchy, light and adapted for small hands, they are ideal for home, kindergarten or park. They are fun for children, and tasty for adults too.

sections.subtitle: Odrasla odgovornost i postavljanje granica u odgoju: Zašto je važno da roditelj bude 'odrasli Marko'
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava razliku između dječjeg i odraslog ponašanja.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević naglašava ključnu ulogu odrasle osobe u odgoju. Ističe da, ako dijete 'ludi', odrasla osoba treba biti ta s više iskustva, znanja i mudrosti koja se može nositi sa situacijom. Objašnjava da se djeca ne znaju nositi s emocijama, ali da ni mnogi odrasli to ne znaju. Razlika je u tome što se dječje ponašanje često definira kao 'neodgojeno', 'bezobrazno' ili 'razmaženo', dok se slični ispadi kod odraslih percipiraju drugačije.

Marko uspoređuje Božji odgoj s ljudskim, ističući da je Bog neumoran u objašnjavanju i usmjeravanju, unatoč ljudskim pogreškama. Roditelji ne smiju dopustiti da ih dječje nezadovoljstvo 'trigerira' na vlastite rane iz djetinjstva, poput osjećaja 'ja to nikad nisam imao, a on je nezadovoljan'. Prisjeća se kako je kao 'odrasli Marko' vodio konferencije za stotine ljudi, dok se 'mali Marko' (njegovo unutarnje dijete) borio s dvoje djece, podsjećajući ga na ulogu starijeg brata koji se morao brinuti o sestrama. To ga je naučilo da je analiza vlastitog unutarnjeg stanja ključna.

Postavljanje granica je neophodno. Marko citira sina: 'Uvijek je po vašem, po mom smije biti samo kad ti odlučiš da može biti po mom', na što on odgovara da je on roditelj. Djeca moraju dobiti otpor i upoznati sve emocije, uključujući ljutnju. Normalna reakcija na proliveno piće je frustracija, ali nakon toga slijedi komunikacija i objašnjenje. Važno je učiti djecu da prepoznaju emocije (ljutnja, tuga, nervoza, umor) i postaviti pravila ponašanja (ne bacati hranu, pospremiti nered). Također, ograničava određene igračke poput flomastera i plastelina zbog nereda. Zaključuje da djeca moraju naučiti da neće svi tolerirati njihovo ponašanje u životu.
sections.subtitle_en: Adult responsibility and setting boundaries in parenting: Why it is important for a parent to be 'adult Marko'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains the difference between childish and adult behavior.
sections.content_en:
Marko Matijašević emphasizes the key role of an adult in upbringing. He points out that, if a child 'goes crazy', the adult should be the one with more experience, knowledge, and wisdom who can handle the situation. He explains that children do not know how to deal with emotions, but that many adults do not know this either. The difference is that children's behavior is often defined as 'ill-mannered', 'rude', or 'spoiled', while similar outbursts in adults are perceived differently.

Marko compares God's upbringing with human upbringing, emphasizing that God is tireless in explaining and guiding, despite human errors. Parents must not allow children's dissatisfaction to 'trigger' them to their own childhood wounds, such as the feeling 'I never had that, and he is dissatisfied'. He recalls how as 'adult Marko' he led conferences for hundreds of people, while 'little Marko' (his inner child) struggled with two children, reminding him of the role of an older brother who had to take care of his sisters. This taught him that analyzing one's own inner state is crucial.

Setting boundaries is essential. Marko quotes his son: 'It's always your way, my way can only be when you decide it can be my way', to which he replies that he is the parent. Children must encounter resistance and get to know all emotions, including anger. A normal reaction to a spilled drink is frustration, but after that, communication and explanation follow. It is important to teach children to recognize emotions (anger, sadness, nervousness, tiredness) and to set rules of behavior (do not throw food, clean up the mess). Also, he limits certain toys like markers and playdough due to the mess. He concludes that children must learn that not everyone will tolerate their behavior in life.

sections.subtitle: Dječji egocentrizam i važnost komunikacije roditeljskih sukoba
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava kako djeca doživljavaju roditeljske svađe.
sections.content:
Voditeljica ističe da su djeca prirodno egocentrična i sebična, te da sebi pripisuju sve zasluge, ali i krivnju. Marko Matijašević potvrđuje važnost rečenice 'Nisi ti kriv' nakon roditeljskih svađa. Sjeća se da on sam nikada nije čuo tu rečenicu od svojih roditelja, što je rezultiralo osjećajem krivnje u odrasloj dobi. Stoga, on i njegova supruga Mirjana, čak i kad se posvađaju pred djecom, uvijek im kažu: 'Mama i tata se vole, trenutno se ne slažemo oko nečega, pa smo malo glasnije razgovarali, ali sve je ok. Nisi ti kriv.'

Marko naglašava da je važno propitkivati se i davati najbolje od sebe u odgoju, ali ne težiti savršenstvu. Smatra da je ključno biti siguran da u danom trenutku daješ najbolje od sebe, a ako postoji prostor za poboljšanje, raditi na tome, umjesto da se obeshrabruješ zbog nesavršenosti.
sections.subtitle_en: Children's egocentrism and the importance of communication of parental conflicts
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains how children experience parental arguments.
sections.content_en: The host points out that children are naturally egocentric and selfish, and that they attribute all merits, but also guilt, to themselves. Marko Matijašević confirms the importance of the sentence 'It's not your fault' after parental arguments. He remembers that he himself never heard that sentence from his parents, which resulted in a feeling of guilt in adulthood. Therefore, he and his wife Mirjana, even when they argue in front of the children, always tell them: 'Mom and Dad love each other, we currently don't agree on something, so we talked a bit louder, but everything is okay. It's not your fault.' Marko emphasizes that it is important to question oneself and give one's best in upbringing, but not to strive for perfection. He believes that it is crucial to be sure that you are giving your best at any given moment, and if there is room for improvement, to work on it, instead of being discouraged by imperfections.

sections.subtitle: Uključivanje djece u obiteljski život: Djeca kao dio, a ne ograničenje
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o putovanjima s djecom.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević i njegova supruga Mirjana aktivno uključuju djecu u sve aspekte obiteljskog života, odbijajući da im djeca ograniče život. Ističu da djeca dolaze u njihov život i obitelj, a ne obrnuto. Kao primjere navodi putovanja u Milano i Rim, gdje su, unatoč izazovima poput dječjeg valjanja po podu i plakanja, djeca pratila tempo obitelji. Marko naglašava da djeca nisu hendikep, već dio obitelji, te da je sve moguće s njima, iako je zahtjevnije.

Posebno ističe važnost odlaska na misu svake nedjelje, čak i ako to znači putovanje 25 km do crkve s kriptom gdje djeca mogu hodati, kako ne bi ometali druge. Smatra da je to bolje nego očekivati od sedmogodišnjaka da mirno sjedi u klupi ako nikada prije nije bio u crkvi. Marko također naglašava da je partner najvažnija osoba u obitelji, ispred djece i roditelja, jer će djeca jednog dana otići, a odnos s partnerom ostaje. Voditeljica dodaje da izlaganje djece iskustvima znači i izlaganje roditelja frustracijama, ali da je to važno za odnos, unatoč tome što je lakše ostati doma i pustiti djecu pred TV.
sections.subtitle_en: Inclusion of children in family life: Children as a part, and not a limitation
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević talks about travels with children.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević and his wife Mirjana actively involve children in all aspects of family life, refusing to let their children limit their lives. They emphasize that children come into their life and family, not the other way around. As examples, he cites trips to Milano and Rim, where, despite challenges like children rolling on the floor and crying, the children followed the family's pace. Marko emphasizes that children are not a handicap, but a part of the family, and that everything is possible with them, although it is more demanding. He particularly highlights the importance of going to Mass every Sunday, even if it means traveling 25 km to a church with a crypt where children can walk, so as not to disturb others. He believes that this is better than expecting a seven-year-old to sit quietly in a pew if they have never been to church before. Marko also emphasizes that the partner is the most important person in the family, ahead of children and parents, because children will one day leave, and the relationship with the partner remains. The host adds that exposing children to experiences also means exposing parents to frustrations, but that this is important for the relationship, despite it being easier to stay home and let the children watch TV.

sections.subtitle: Utjecaj tehnologije i društva na djecu: Kako naučiti djecu prosuđivati
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević razmišlja o utjecaju tehnologije na djecu.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević i njegova supruga Mirjana donijeli su odluku o preseljenju na selo kako bi djeci pružili mirnije okruženje i dvorište, unatoč Markovoj ranijoj preferenciji za gradski život. Smatra da je ključno naučiti djecu *kako prosuđivati* situacije i informacije, umjesto da im se definira svaka odluka. Time će biti spremni za izazove svijeta, bez obzira na to što im se servira.

Marko ističe da djecu ne mogu izolirati od svijeta i tehnologije. Kao primjer navodi pjesmu 'Ceca' koju su djeca čula u katoličkom vrtiću. Umjesto zabrane, važno je objasniti zašto nešto nije dobro. Priznaje da se ni odrasli ne nose dobro s tehnologijom, poput stalnog provjeravanja maila, što mu pomaže razumjeti koliko tehnologija može obuzeti djecu. Stoga su postavili jasna pravila: pola sata igrica subotom i 20 minuta crtića dnevno, koristeći pješčani sat kao vizualni podsjetnik. Vjeruje da će djeca vrijednosti pokupiti iz ponašanja roditelja, a ne samo iz riječi.
sections.subtitle_en: Impact of technology and society on children: How to teach children to discern
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević thinks about the influence of technology on children.
sections.content_en:
Marko Matijašević and his wife Mirjana made the decision to move to the countryside to provide their children with a calmer environment and a yard, despite Marko's earlier preference for city life. He believes it is crucial to teach children *how to judge* situations and information, instead of defining every decision for them. This way they will be ready for the challenges of the world, regardless of what is served to them.

Marko points out that they cannot isolate children from the world and technology. As an example, he mentions the song 'Ceca' which the children heard in a Catholic kindergarten. Instead of prohibition, it is important to explain why something is not good. He admits that even adults do not cope well with technology, such as constantly checking email, which helps him understand how much technology can overwhelm children. Therefore, they set clear rules: half an hour of games on Saturdays and 20 minutes of cartoons daily, using an hourglass as a visual reminder. He believes that children will pick up values from their parents' behavior, and not just from words.

sections.subtitle: Primjerom pokazivanje vrijednosti: Priča o Jamnici i integritetu
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević priča anegdotu iz dućana.
sections.content: Marko Matijašević dijeli anegdotu iz dućana koja ilustrira važnost pokazivanja vrijednosti primjerom. Nakon kupovine, shvatio je da je zaboravio platiti paket Jamnice koji mu je ostao na kolicima. Unatoč tome što je već bio u autu s djecom i žurilo mu se, vratio se u dućan. Blagajnica je bila iznenađena, pitajući ga zašto se vraća zbog paketa Jamnice. Marko joj je objasnio da to čini jer ga djeca gledaju i želi im pokazati dobar primjer. Naglašava da ne može očekivati od djece da rade nešto ispravno ako on sam radi suprotno. Ova situacija pokazuje kako se integritet i poštenje prenose na djecu kroz konkretne postupke.
sections.subtitle_en: Demonstrating values by example: The story of Jamnica and integrity
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević tells an anecdote from the shop.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević shares an anecdote from a store that illustrates the importance of showing values by example. After shopping, he realized that he had forgotten to pay for a pack of Jamnica that remained on his cart. Despite already being in the car with his children and being in a hurry, he returned to the store. The cashier was surprised, asking him why he was returning because of a pack of Jamnica. Marko explained to her that he was doing it because his children were watching him and he wanted to show them a good example. He emphasizes that he cannot expect children to do something right if he himself does the opposite. This situation shows how integrity and honesty are passed on to children through concrete actions.

sections.subtitle: Povjerenje u odgoj i dječji izbori: Dopustiti djeci da prođu svoj 'križni put'
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o Mariji pod križem.
sections.content:
Voditeljica izražava povjerenje da će rano usađeni odnos i vrijednosti pomoći djeci da se nose s lošim utjecajima okoline i vršnjaka. Marko Matijašević se slaže, ali naglašava da djeca imaju slobodu izbora i da roditelji ne mogu djecu 'staviti u balon' i zaštititi ih od svega. Povezuje to s četvrtom postajom križnog puta, kada Isus susreće Mariju. Roditelji, poput Marije, moraju pustiti djecu da prođu svoj križni put, da pate, padaju i muče se. To je bolno iskustvo za roditelja, ali nužno za djetetov rast i pobjedu. Marko ističe da je Marijina bol pod križem primjer te roditeljske patnje.

Nakon križnog puta, ništa nije isto, sve je drugačije. Marko mora biti svjestan da nije spasitelj, već da postoji jedan Spasitelj. Djeca će tražiti svoj identitet i htjeti biti dio nečega, a roditelji ih moraju pustiti da to iskuse. Spominje i važnost životnih odluka koje se donose između pete i sedme godine, naglašavajući da je važno slušati što dijete govori i koje zaključke donosi. Vjeruje da će se susresti s teškim situacijama i da će morati pustiti djecu da sama prođu i pobijede, koliko god ih želi zaštititi, jer je to nužno za njihov rast.
sections.subtitle_en: Trust in upbringing and children's choices: To allow children to go through their 'Way of the Cross'
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about Mary under the cross.
sections.content_en: The host expresses confidence that the early instilled relationship and values will help children cope with bad influences from the environment and peers. Marko Matijašević agrees, but emphasizes that children have freedom of choice and that parents cannot 'put children in a bubble' and protect them from everything. He connects this with the fourth station of the Way of the Cross, when Jesus meets Mary. Parents, like Mary, must let children go through their Way of the Cross, to suffer, fall, and struggle. This is a painful experience for a parent, but necessary for the child's growth and victory. Marko points out that Mary's pain under the cross is an example of that parental suffering. After the Way of the Cross, nothing is the same, everything is different. Marko must be aware that he is not a savior, but that there is one Savior. Children will seek their identity and want to be part of something, and parents must let them experience that. He also mentions the importance of life decisions made between the ages of five and seven, emphasizing that it is important to listen to what the child says and what conclusions they draw. He believes that they will encounter difficult situations and that they will have to let children go through and overcome them themselves, however much he wants to protect them, because it is necessary for their growth.

sections.subtitle: Usađivanje vjere: Želja da djeca vole Boga više od roditelja
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević govori o važnosti vjere za svoju djecu.
sections.content:
Marko Matijašević dijeli svoju duboku želju za duhovnim odgojem svoje djece. Sinu je rekao: 'Želim da voliš Isusa više nego mene. Da ga slušaš više nego mene.' Objasnio mu je da, ako Isus kaže jedno, a on drugo, treba slušati Isusa. Marko ističe da mu je sada potpuno jasan citat: 'Tko ne ljubi mene više od oca, ali majke nije dostojan.' Smatra da bi njegov najveći uspjeh u roditeljstvu bio kada bi djeca voljela Isusa više od njega.

Potiče djecu da služe Jahvi, poput Jošue, govoreći im: 'Ja i moj dom, ja ti, ja mama, mi služimo Jahvi, ono mi želimo služiti Jahvi, ono želim da ti isto tako služi.' Kroz takve razgovore i primjere, Marko želi da djeca upiju to iskustvo i odnos s Bogom, da budu svjesna što Bog čini u njihovom životu i da budu dio toga.
sections.subtitle_en: Instilling faith: The desire that children love God more than parents
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević speaks about the importance of faith for his children.
sections.content_en: Marko Matijašević shares his deep desire for the spiritual upbringing of his children. He told his son: 'I want you to love Jesus more than me. To listen to Him more than me.' He explained to him that, if Jesus says one thing, and he another, he should listen to Jesus. Marko emphasizes that the quote is now completely clear to him: 'Whoever does not love me more than father, but of mother is not worthy.' He believes that his greatest success in parenting would be if his children loved Jesus more than him. He encourages his children to serve Yahweh, like Joshua, telling them: 'I and my household, I you, I mom, we serve Yahweh, that we want to serve Yahweh, that I want you to serve likewise.' Through such conversations and examples, Marko wants the children to absorb that experience and relationship with God, to be aware of what God does in their lives and to be part of it.

sections.subtitle: Definicija 'dobrog čovjeka': Gledati druge Božjim očima
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević razmišlja o definiciji 'dobrog čovjeka'.
sections.content:
Na pitanje voditeljice što za njega znači biti dobar čovjek, Marko Matijašević odgovara da je to prvenstveno gledati druge ljude iz Božje perspektive, prepoznajući da je svatko vrijedan. Podsjeća da je Isus umro za svakoga, bez obzira na pogreške ili 'loše stvari'. Biti dobar čovjek, prema Marku, znači prihvatiti Božju ljubav za sebe i onda s tom ljubavlju gledati druge ljude, bez obzira na to gdje se oni nalaze u životu.

Naglašava da to ne znači tolerirati zlostavljanje ili ostati u lošem odnosu, već imati poštovanje prema drugoj osobi. Marko ističe da patnja nije uvijek 'križ' od Boga, niti Bog želi kažnjavati. Umjesto toga, život donosi poteškoće i bol, ali Bog je u tim trenucima blizu, pruža utjehu i nosi nas. Zaključuje da biti dobar čovjek znači imati želju činiti dobro, postavljati se s poštovanjem prema drugima i vidjeti dobro u svakome, ne zatomljujući pritom vlastito mišljenje.
sections.subtitle_en: Definition of a 'good person': To look at others with God's eyes
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević thinks about the definition of 'good person'.
sections.content_en: To the host's question what it means for him to be a good person, Marko Matijašević answers that it is primarily to look at other people from God's perspective, recognizing that everyone is valuable. He reminds that Jesus died for everyone, regardless of mistakes or 'bad things'. To be a good person, according to Marko, means to accept God's love for oneself and then with that love to look at other people, regardless of where they are in life. He emphasizes that this does not mean tolerating abuse or staying in a bad relationship, but rather having respect for the other person. Marko points out that suffering is not always a 'cross' from God, nor does God want to punish. Instead, life brings difficulties and pain, but God is close in those moments, provides comfort, and carries us. He concludes that to be a good person means to have a desire to do good, to position oneself with respect towards others and to see good in everyone, without suppressing one's own opinion.

sections.subtitle: Predavanje 'Duraj, pa uraj': Razlika između križa i tereta
sections.screenshot_description: Marko Matijašević objašnjava naslov predavanja 'Duraj, pa uraj'.
sections.content:
Voditeljica pita Marka Matijaševića o nazivu njegovog predavanja 'Duraj, pa uraj' (Izdrži, pa uspij). Marko objašnjava da naslov proizlazi iz činjenice da ljudi često nose i terete i križeve, ne znajući razliku. Ističe da je važno prepoznati koje stvari treba pustiti, koje su križevi koje nam Bog donosi, a koje su tereti koje sami sebi namećemo.

Marko se osvrće na perspektivu nekih ljudi koji osjećaju da ih Bog samo kažnjava i oduzima im dobro. Predlaže promjenu perspektive: možda se, unatoč lomovima i nestajanju stvari, Bog i dalje nalazi blizu. Bog nije zločinac koji nas maltretira; život se jednostavno događa, donoseći poteškoće, bol i patnju. No, u svemu tome, Bog je blizu, dobar je, ljubav je, vjeran je i nosi nas. Poruka je jasna: 'Doći ćemo ako ne odustanemo.'
sections.subtitle_en: Lecture 'Endure, then do it': Difference between cross and burden
sections.screenshot_description_en: Marko Matijašević explains the title of the lecture 'Duraj, pa uraj'.
sections.content_en: The host asks Marko Matijašević about the title of his lecture 'Duraj, pa uraj' (Endure, then succeed). Marko explains that the title stems from the fact that people often carry both burdens and crosses, not knowing the difference. He points out that it is important to recognize which things should be let go of, which are crosses that God brings us, and which are burdens that we impose on ourselves. Marko reflects on the perspective of some people who feel that God only punishes them and takes away their good. He suggests a change of perspective: perhaps, despite breakdowns and things disappearing, God is still close. God is not a criminal who abuses us; life simply happens, bringing difficulties, pain, and suffering. However, in all of that, God is close, He is good, He is love, He is faithful, and He carries us. The message is clear: 'We will arrive if we do not give up.'

iterations.theme_en: Parental challenges, upbringing and spiritual values
